Brand Guidelines · v0.1 (working draft)

Brand Guidelines

Everything you need to produce on-brand materials for the ITS Africa Conference — Cape Town, 9–10 March 2027. Use the assets exactly as supplied. You never need to choose a colour, font or layout: it's already decided here.

Cape Town 9–10 March 2027
01 — Logo

The logo

The "its" mark with the Africa Conference lockup. It comes in three layouts. Pick the one that fits your space; use the version made for your background.

Landscape

Wide spaces: banners, email headers, website.

Portrait

Tall/narrow spaces: posters, roll-ups, stories.

Square / stacked

Social avatars, tight square placements.

Backgrounds — use the right version

On white

On cream

On dark

On brand blue

Do

  • Keep clear space around the logo — at least the height of the "i" dot on all sides.
  • Use the black version on light backgrounds, the white version on dark/photo backgrounds.
  • Scale it proportionally.

Don't

  • Don't stretch, squash, rotate or recolour it (colour versions are coming separately).
  • Don't put the black logo on a dark background or a busy photo.
  • Don't rebuild or retype the lockup — always use the supplied file.

Coming soon: approved colour logo versions. Until then, use black or white.

02 — Colour

Colour palette

Drawn from the conference's African geometric pattern. Blue is the primary brand colour; gold is a highlight only. Click any swatch to copy its hex.

Do

  • Backgrounds: cream or white. Text: black or dark grey.
  • Use white text on blue, green or terracotta fills.
  • One accent colour per item — let the pattern carry the full-colour energy.

Don't

  • Don't set body text in gold on white (too low contrast) — gold is for highlights only.
  • Don't use all six colours at once in a layout.
  • Don't put a colour behind body text.

Values are working references pending final confirmation from ITS Global. RGB shown for screen; ask us for print (CMYK/Pantone).

03 — Typography

Typography

One typeface: Montserrat — free from Google Fonts. Used exactly as the ITS World Congress family uses it. If Montserrat isn't installed, Word and PowerPoint fall back to Arial and still look right.

Headline — Montserrat Black / Bold, tight
Connect. Deploy. Move Africa.
Subheading — Montserrat SemiBold
Cape Town · 9–10 March 2027
Body — Montserrat Regular / Medium
This is body copy. Keep it black or dark grey on a light background, around 16px, with comfortable line spacing. Montserrat Regular for running text, Medium for light emphasis.
Label / eyebrow — Montserrat SemiBold, uppercase
Programme · Speakers · Partners

Headline colour options

Montserrat stays the same — only the colour changes. Here's how a headline reads in each brand colour. Body copy always stays black or dark grey; these options are for headlines and short statements. Tell me which you'd like as the default.

Get the font (free)

Download Montserrat from Google Fonts and install it, or in Office just pick "Montserrat" if it's available. No account or licence needed.

fonts.google.com/specimen/Montserrat

Fallback

Templates are set to fall back to Arial if Montserrat isn't installed, so nothing ever breaks. Don't substitute other fonts.

04 — Pattern

The pattern

The African geometric border is the conference's signature device. Use it as a horizontal band — a header/footer strip, banner edge or slide divider — never as a full background behind text.

Placeholder: the band above is an indicative stand-in in the brand colours. The final high-resolution pattern is being prepared and will replace this, with corner and divider crops for banners, slides and social.

05 — Partner lockup

Organiser & partner lockup

The standard footer lockup for official communications: Organised by, Hosted by, Content Partner. Use this exact arrangement and spacing.

Organised by
Hosted by
Content Partner
06 — Downloads

Grab the files

Logo exports and the organiser banner live in the project's assets folder. Open the file you need for your background and layout.
